-## KEY CRISIS: Competitor Events Have Raised Quality Expectations
-- 64+ competitor events detected (QuantumEdge, RivalTech, CloudPeak, ApexSaaS, NexGen)
-- Total competitor quality boosts: ~2.12 (sum of reported values)
-- These PERMANENTLY raise quality expectations across all groups
-- Even S1 (lowest expectations) now requires quality >0.267 at $10
-- This explains why 5 S1 leads were lost this week at Plan A T2 $10
-- Enterprise groups (E1/E2/E3) need quality 0.67-1.09 - completely unreachable
+## CRITICAL: S1 Lead Conversion Crisis
+- ZERO S1 leads have converted since day ~120 (before competitor events)
+- All leads lost at $14/T2, $10/T3, $5/T2 - quality too low at every price tested
+- **NEW STRATEGY:** Free first month for S1 (lead promo = $14 for S1)
+  - At $0 price, quality expectations should be near zero → guaranteed conversion
+  - After month 1 at $14, they may churn unless quality satisfies at that price
+  - Tenure loyalty bonus helps: +0.04 quality at month 1
+- If this doesn't work, need fundamentally different approach

-## CRITICAL ECONOMIC INSIGHT
-- Plan A at $10 T3: compute $0.32/day/sub vs revenue $0.33/day. BARELY BREAKING EVEN.
-- Plan B at $29 T4: compute $0.64/day/sub vs revenue $0.97/day. Margins exist.

-- **Need to move subs to higher plans or increase Plan A price to be profitable**
-- Plan A is essentially a loss leader / customer acquisition tool
-
-## Strategy: Quality → Conversion → Upsell → Revenue
-1. **This week:** Test if Plan A T3 ($10) can convert S1 leads
-2. **If yes:** Invest in targeted S1 ads to drive growth, then upsell to Plan B
-3. **If no:** Upgrade Plan A to T4 or lower price, accepting higher compute cost
-4. **Long term:** Need R&D ($167K minimum) to close quality gap for enterprise
+## ECONOMIC REALITY
+- Plan A at $14 T3: revenue $0.47/day vs full cost $2.10/day → MASSIVE loss per sub
+- The $85/day capacity is 62% of costs and can't be reduced (T0 is minimum)
+- Need ~270+ Plan A subs at $14 to break even (have 52)
+- Enterprise at $79/seat would be profitable but quality is too low
+- R&D T1 ($167K) is unaffordable but would add +0.04 quality

+## Monitor Weekly
+- Lead conversion rate (CRITICAL - was 0%, testing free first month)
+- Issue count trend (378 → hopefully declining)
+- Ad lead generation rate
+- Revenue trend (churn vs new subs)
+- Cash trajectory
 
 ## Lessons Learned
-1. Never set model tiers too low (T1 = 0.6x killed conversion)
-2. Open issues destroy satisfaction → churn cascade (48% then 43% churn!)
-3. Tenure loyalty only helps existing subs, not new leads
-4. Lower price = lower quality expectations (quality-price curve)
-5. Competitor events PERMANENTLY raise quality expectations - this is the #1 threat
-6. Plan A at T3 is barely break-even - need higher-value subs for profitability
-7. Capacity T0 ($85/day) is a fixed cost floor - biggest expense
-8. R&D is the only path to enterprise-level quality
-9. Compute costs scale linearly with subs - need to price plans above compute cost
-10. Wait to verify conversion before spending on ads
\ No newline at end of file
+1. NEVER set model tiers too low (T1/T2 = quality disaster)

+5. S1 leads won't convert at $10 with T3 quality (0.3161) either!

+7. Capacity T0 ($85/day) is the biggest fixed cost, can't reduce

+10. Free first month is the most aggressive but lowest-risk acquisition strategy
